name: Snow Report 2026-03-19 16:00:10
report:
Good evening, Beast fans,

We had flurries fall sporadically throughout the day to give us a wintry feel here in mid-March. Thanks to the efforts of our grooming team, our surfaces made an improvement, and conditions should continue to get better overnight. 

Snow rolls in tomorrow, turning heavier by late afternoon with a mix of snow, sleet, and rain as temps climb into the mid-30s. Expect breezy conditions and about 1–2 inches of accumulation before things transition.

Surfaces should continue to improve with a slight warm up tomorrow and with another night of grooming, we should be in decent shape. Our grooming team is after a similar plan and will be swapping Dipper for Downdraft. Other runs that will be groomed by the morning are Cruise Control, Bear Claw, Reason, Bunny Buster, and over 80 others. With plans and conditions subject to change this time of year, be sure to check out the Lifts & Trails Report to help you scope out where to head. 

Our snowmaking team took advantage of the cold stretch this week, firing on Snowshed Slope, Upper Skyelark, Lower Bittersweet, and Highlander, along with fan guns around K-1, Snowdon, and Mouse Trap. We’re taking a short break from snowmaking but there could be another window at the beginning of next week so stay tuned! Check out our latest Insider Blog to see how recent snowmaking investments have been paying off (spoiler alert: they’re paying off big time). 

In our kparks, Dream Maker is firing with 16 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain. The Stash is the place to be for a more natural feel, with plenty of creative stone and wooden features on the trail and many more in the woods. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. 

Finally, if you cannot get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199.

Hope your Friday is a fun one! 

-Evan

 

Operating Day: 129

Lifts         

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.        

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.         

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!         

The Tubing Park presented by bubly remains open Friday - Sunday through March 22.    

Uphill Travel         

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route.      

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.                        

MOUNTAIN SERVICES       

Parking & Shuttles       

Parking is available at the K-1, Snowshed, Ramshead, Bear, Skyeship and Vale parking lots. Please respect signage and allow our parking staff to assist you on your trip to the slopes. Shuttle service is available from 7:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. midweek and 6:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekends and holidays.       

Winter car camping and overnight parking is only available in the lot across from Skyeship Lodge. See details here.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-19 06:00:57
report:
Good morning, Beast fans,

Some hard surfaces didn’t stop us from enjoying the skiing & riding yesterday as we kept the shred going. We wrapped up our Michelob ULTRA Race Series with some fiercely competitive dual slalom races on Highline. Thank you to everyone who competed this winter and made the Ski Bum community a great one. 

Mostly cloudy skies stick around today, with highs near 35 degrees at the base and 28 at the peak. A steady south wind keeps things feeling sharp, so come prepared for a slightly chilly ride out there. 

Temperatures are still great for snowmaking, and the crew continues to run the guns on Snowshed Slope, Upper Skyelark, Lower Bittersweet, and Highlander, along with fan guns around K-1, Snowdon, and Mouse Trap. They're also looking to make some line repairs on the Learn to Ski & Ride Area, which should hopefully be coming back online shortly. Our snowgun count is currently over 100, which is quite the feat this late in the season! A big thank you to our snowmaking team for extending the season for everyone. 

Surface conditions have been firm, and we can expect more of that today while groomed trails improve with another resurface. Other than machine groomed hardpack, the slopes will consist of loose granular. The grooming team added Outer Limits to the plan overnight for another steep option among smooth runs. Trails such as Skyelark, Rime, Chute, and many more also got a groom. With plans and conditions subject to change this time of year, be sure to check out the Lifts & Trails Report to help you scope out where to head. 

In our kparks, Dream Maker is firing with 16 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain. The Stash is the place to be for a more natural feel, with plenty of creative stone and wooden features on the trail and many more in the woods. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. 

Finally, if you cannot get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199.

Another day at The Beast is a good day! 

-Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-18 05:59:40
report:
Good morning, Beast fans,

Even though conditions were challenging yesterday, we're still lucky to be sliding on snow, and we’re thankful for our team for making it happen. The pot of gold was here all along. 

Winter is back again for the 10th time over the past two weeks. It’s been a roller coaster of freeze-thaw cycles, but it appears temperatures will stay relatively cold until Friday, when things start to trend warmer. Last night, temperatures dropped into the single digits, and you better believe our snowmaking team is taking advantage of it. By the time lifts open, temps are expected to climb out of the single digits and rise consistently through the day, reaching the low 20s by the afternoon. Winds will remain strong in the morning, with gusts up to 40 mph, but it will calm down as the day goes on. Plus, we’re expecting a healthy dose of sunshine, which will make the day all the more enjoyable.

With temps right where we want them, snowmaking will continue full-blast over the next couple of days. Our crews have things up and running on Snowshed Slope, Upper Skyelark, Lower Bittersweet, Highlander, along with fan guns around K-1, Snowdon, and Mouse Trap. They're also looking to make some line repairs on the Learn to Ski & Ride Area, which should hopefully be coming back online shortly. Our snowgun count should continue to climb to well over 100 throughout the day! Shout out to our snowmaking team for extending the season for all ability levels. 

Conditions became increasingly firm over the course of St. Patrick's Day, and we expect them to stay on the firm side for today. Machine groomed hard pack and areas of loose granular are your primary conditions you'll see out there, but our grooming team will ensure that the slopes are riding nice and smooth despite the firm surfaces. They added plenty of groomed runs into their plan last night including Pipe Dream from top to bottom, Needles Eye, Lower Wildfire and Upper Chute down through the top portion of Great Bear. With plans and conditions subject to change this time of year, be sure to check out the Lifts & Trails Report to help you scope out where to head. 

Please note that Highline will be closed for the majority of the day for the Michelob ULTRA Ski Bum Finals. Catch the action right down in the K-1 Base Area to watch some intense dual slalom racing!

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun beginner-friendly features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. The Stash and Dream Maker are expected to reopen today, with our grooming team and Park Crew shaping them overnight. Check the Terrain Parks Report for the latest updates.

Finally, if you cannot get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199.

Spring is on its way, but for now, think snow!

-Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-17 05:40:46
report:
Good morning, Beast fans,

Happy St. Patrick's Day! While we're feeling lucky to be at The Beast, I don't think we'll be finding that pot of gold today. Key lifts will have a delayed opening this morning at 9:30 a.m. to allow grooming to be completed and lifts to be deiced. As always, check the Lifts & Trails Report once we open for the latest updates and any potential wind-related changes.

The weather has been funky overnight as temps shifted from the high 40s down to below freezing this Tuesday morning. We experienced some wet precipitation overnight, which transitioned to a light mix and a dusting of snow. We'll start the day with mostly cloudy skies, and we're expecting the sun to pop out later in the day. Temperatures at the peak slipped below freezing shortly after midnight and they will continue to fall today. It'll be around 15 degrees up top by the time lifts open, dropping down into the single digits by the end of the day. 

West winds of 30 to 55 mph with higher gusts are expected right around opening, which could mean a few wind holds early. The good news is that winds should settle down as the day goes on. If you feel like sleeping in a bit this morning, it might not be the worst call. That said, we will still be out there getting our turns in.

With the significant shift in temps and weather, our grooming team planned a delayed start to their shift, starting around midnight to let our tractors get a better bite in the snow. Crews focused their efforts on the Northside of the mountain first before moving over to Needles Eye and Bear Mountain where they are dealing with ice flows. We'll have more details on grooming and terrain status before we open, and be sure to check out the Lifts & Trails Report throughout the day to help you scope out where to head.

If you are planning to get going right at opening, starting your day at K-1, Snowshed, or Ramshead will be your safest play while work continues on the Southside. Skiing and riding conditions will be variable due to the weather overnight and rapidly changing temps. We're expecting frozen granular and machine groomed hard pack, with potential for glazing on the surface. Anything not groomed overnight is slick, and will likely not ski today. Conditions underneath the surface are still wet, but we expect things to firm up over the course of the day. Ski Patrol will be assessing conditions throughout the day to see what can safely remain open. 

Now for the good news. Snowmaking makes it's return today. Our crews will be charging the system back up and working from the bottom of the mountain upward to capitalize on the colder air down low first. Snowshed and the Learn to Ski & Ride Area should be the first areas to come online, with additional terrain following as temperatures continue to drop. We will be building coverage in key zones including Skyelark to Bittersweet, along with fan guns around K-1, Snowdon, and Mouse Trap.

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun beginner-friendly features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. The Stash and Dream Maker will be closed today while conditions reset and grooming resumes in those zones. They will reopen when conditions allow. Check the Terrain Parks Report for the latest updates.

Finally, if you cannot get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199.

While it may be a little spicy weather-wise, we’re still going to the most of it! If the skiing and riding isn't your treat, check out our delicious St. Patrick's Day food specials across the mountain. At Snowshed and Ramshead, we'll have Guinness Beef Stew in a bread bowl on special. Also on Tuesday In the Long Trail Pub, try our corned beef brisket, cabbage, potatoes, carrots & seeded mustard. K-1 will offer a shaved corn beef melt at the deli with sauerkraut, Swiss cheese, and whole grain mustard spread. We have a Corned Beef Ruben Sandwich planned at Bear Mountain as well. Enjoy!

Be safe and have fun. See you on the mountain!

-Austin

name: Snow Report 2026-03-16 16:12:56
report:
Good evening, Beast fans,

The weather will continue to be a little nasty tonight as this system moves through, bringing a little more of that nonsense wet stuff along with strong winds. Temperatures at the peak should slip below freezing shortly after midnight and continue dropping overnight, with tomorrow’s highs topping out around 15° at the summit and about 20° at the base.

Tomorrow morning may not be the earliest start to your ski day. West winds of 30 to 55 mph with higher gusts are expected right around opening, which could mean a few wind holds early. The good news is that winds should settle down as the day goes on. If you feel like sleeping in a bit tomorrow morning, it might not be the worst call. That said, we will still be out there getting our turns in.

With tonight transitioning to colder temperatures, grooming will begin once temps drop below freezing, letting our tractors get a better bite in the snow. Crews will get rolling after midnight as conditions allow, focusing on the Northside of the mountain first before moving over to Bear Mountain. That means terrain over at Bear could see a delayed opening while grooming wraps up in that zone. The goal is around 77 groomed trails, and these cord runs will likely be the best bet to kick off your day. Be sure to check out the Lifts & Trails Report first thing to help you scope out where to head.

If you are planning to get going right at opening, starting your day at K-1, Snowshed, or Ramshead will be your safest play while work continues on the Southside.

Now for the good news. Snowmaking returns tomorrow. Our crews will be charging the system back up and working from the bottom of the mountain upward to capitalize on the colder air down low first. Snowshed and the ALA should be the first areas to come online, with additional terrain following as temperatures continue to drop. We will be building coverage in key zones including Skyelark to Bittersweet, along with fan guns around K-1, Snowdon, and Mouse Trap.

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun beginner-friendly features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. The Stash and Dream Maker will be closed tomorrow while conditions reset and grooming resumes in those zones. They will reopen when conditions allow. Check the Terrain Parks Report for the latest updates.

Key lifts are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad opening at 9:30 a.m. As always, check the Lifts & Trails Report in the morning for the latest updates and any potential wind-related changes.

Finally, if you cannot get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199.

Tomorrow might start a little spicy weather-wise, but we’ll be out there making the most of it!

See you on the hill.

-Josh

name: Snow Report 2026-03-16 05:39:43
report:
Good morning, Beast fans,

False spring took a break this weekend, as 8” of snow over the last 48 hours allowed us to re-open a number of trails throughout yesterday. Today, the weather takes a bit of a turn for the worse, but there is light at the end of the tunnel: snowmaking will be back on Tuesday! More on that to come.

As for today, a not-so-fun weather system is moving through, bringing warmer temperatures, increased winds, and underdeveloped snowflakes. Temps are hovering right around freezing as the initial precipitation moves in, so there may be a bit of snow at higher elevations early. The incoming warm-up should give our snow surfaces some give first thing this morning, but precipitation may continue in a warmer fashion through the morning as well. Temps are expected to rise through the afternoon, reaching about 50° by the time lifts close.

It’s also going to be a windy one out there today, with south winds running 30–40 mph and potential gusts over 50 mph. Safe to say this may not be the best day of skiing we’ve had this year, but we’ll make the most of it.

Snow conditions were great yesterday. The extra 3” overnight brought on some additional ungroomed terrain for those up for the challenge. Skyehawk offered some gnarly (in a fun way) turns in the afternoon as fresh snow got kicked around. Our groomers worked overnight to maintain snow surfaces through the incoming weather, doing what they do best: getting the best possible product out there for skiing and riding. Surfaces should become spring-like again by this afternoon if you prefer softer snow underfoot.

On the grooming front, our team tackled around 80 trails overnight, and those will be the safest bet right at opening this morning. Downdraft and Cascade received winch grooming overnight if you prefer the steeps. For a nice cruise, Upper Pipe Dream is always a dreamy trek. Cruise Control also held a great surface yesterday even as the fresh snow moved around through the afternoon. See our Lifts & Trails Report for the full list of groomed runs.

We have 15 lifts scheduled to spin today, but do check that Lifts & Trails Report for any potential wind-related changes. Strong southerly winds could have an effect on some of our non-fixed-grip lifts throughout the day.

Back to the good news: our snowmakers will be out in force this Tuesday, as The Beast’s snowguns roar back to life once again. We’ll be laying down coverage in key areas around the mountain, from Bear to Snowdon and plenty in between. Check back here tomorrow for more specifics on which trails will be receiving some love.

Up in our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard Parks offering beginner-friendly features for all. Timberline remains jam-packed with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. At Bear Mountain, Dream Maker continues to offer big air and big thrills with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base of Bear. The Stash also brings along fun natural features to our lengthy feature list, as well as a couple more jumps. Check out our Terrain Parks Report for full details.

Finally, if you can’t get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to a Spring Pass at any time for just $199.

Have a great day out there.

-Josh

name: Snow Report 2026-03-15 05:42:32
report:
Good morning, Beast fans!

Winter is back in full swing today as The Beast has become a winter wonderland once again. The mountain as covered in 8” of powder from the past 48 hours, with an additional 3" coming in last night. With Friday night's snow getting packed down by our groomers and last night's snow adding more fluff on top, today should be a fun one out there.

Today will bring more cloud coverage overhead, with temperatures topping out around 30 at the base and 24 at the peak. Expect breezy conditions throughout the afternoon as winds shift and pick up before lifts stop for the evening.

While surface conditions were variable at times yesterday, there was plenty of packed powder and pow pockets to snag. The mountain has seen a huge improvement in snow surfaces since the recent refreeze, and today should be smoother than yesterday as the new snow gets worked in and hard surfaces get softened up. Yesterday, the Canyon had goods in Double Dipper and Downdraft with powdery turns, and I wouldn't be surprised to find more there today.

For groomers, our team added Ovation to the 82 trails that will get a refresh tonight. These are the kinds of days where our Mountain Ops teams work their magic to smooth out any variable conditions out there. In the morning, groomers should be skiing and riding smoothly out there. Bunny Buster should be good option today, and you can’t go wrong with cruisers on Snowdon as most of those runs get regroomed. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report before booting up to get the full rundown. 

Our key lifts are scheduled for 8:00 a.m. openings today, with the Canyon Quad and South Ridge Quad joining the fun at 9:00 a.m. Get those South Ridge laps in while you can! That's where I'll end up sometime this morning.

The forecast is showing some friendly snowmaking temperatures starting on St. Patrick’s Day, so you know what that means: our crew is planning to fire up the snowguns once again, laying down extra support around the mountain to prep snow surfaces even further for the spring. Read our latest Insider Blog to learn how much our recent investments in snowmaking have worked out nicely this season. 

Up in our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard Parks offering beginner-friendly features for all. Timberline remains jam-packed with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. At Bear Mountain, Dream Maker continues to offer big air and big thrills with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base of Bear. The Stash brings along fun natural features to our lengthy feature list, as well as a couple more jumps. Check out our Terrain Parks Report for full details. 

If you can’t get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to a Spring Pass at any time for just $199. 

Let's get after it!

- Cullen
